response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. She needs to calculate how many 14-ounce bottles she needs to last through a leap year. The leap year has 366 days, so she will wash her hair 366 / 2 = 183 times. Each use requires 1/4 ounce of shampoo, so the total amount needed is 183 * 1/4 = 45.75 ounces. Since she cannot buy a fraction of a bottle, she needs to round up to the next whole number, which is 46 ounces.

[address] is the number of 14-ounce bottles needed to provide 46 ounces of shampoo. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so [address] = 46 / 14 = 3.2857. Since she cannot buy a fraction of a bottle, she needs to round up to the next whole number, which is 4. Therefore, she needs 4 bottles to get through a leap year.
